mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, weak labor market conditions signal impending corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2011.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that outweigh demand stimulus, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent directional shift.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price indices reflect expanding nominal economic activity and corporate pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5%, insufficient aggregate demand forces corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the threshold shows no statistical edge in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS rises but UNRATE stagnates, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals resolving supply bottlenecks and improving corporate capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the mechanism performs identically to baseline in non-recovery periods.
